event_headline: OSG opposes Senator Dela Rosa Supreme Court petition to block ICC arrest warrant

chronology.action: admitted regretting lapses in the bloody war on drugs, mentioning convicted police who killed Kian delos Santos
considered Senator Ronald “Bato” Dela Rosa a suspect-at-large
was captured on surveillance footage running down hallways as they chased Philippine Senator Ronald dela Rosa inside the Senate building
called on Senator Ronald “Bato” Dela Rosa to come out of the Senate building and face the case
insisted that he is not like former president Rodrigo Duterte, who reportedly wanted to get arrested and surrender to the International Criminal Court
urged the military to stop government attempts to arrest and fly him to the Netherlands to stand trial on charges of crimes against humanity
added Senator Ronald dela Rosa to its list of wanted persons following the unsealing of an arrest warrant for alleged crimes

chronology.actor: Philippine top organizer of Duterte's drug war (Senator Dela Rosa)
chronology.action: reportedly had 'SHOTS fired' as he tried to resist his arrest, being charged with killing thousands and having an extrajudicial order by the International Criminal Court
stated that an immigration lookout bulletin order (ILBO) cannot prevent Senator Ronald 'Bato' dela Rosa from possibly traveling abroad because it is not equivalent to a hold departure order
issued an immigration lookout bulletin order (ILBO) against Sen. Bato dela Rosa to monitor his possible departure from the country
was described as using the legislature as a shield after being wanted by the ICC for crimes against humanity
called for a review of the background of Senate Sergeant-at-Arms Mao Aplasca, citing his role in the Philippine National Police during the Duterte drug war and linking him to thousands of EJKs
indicated 'Bato' Dela Rosa leaving the Senate with a PNP escort while wanted by the ICC, raising questions about official protection amidst EJK allegations
submitted its comment on the two petitions filed by the camp of Sen. Ronald 'Bato' dela Rosa before the Supreme Court
argued that justice should not forget the “voiceless dead” as it urged the denial of petitions of Senator Ronald 'Bato' dela Rosa before the Supreme Court
asked the Supreme Court to dismiss the petition of Sen. Ronald dela Rosa to block the implementation of the ICC arrest warrant against him

chronology.actor: New lawyer of former President Rodrigo Duterte
chronology.action: requested a re-examination of Duterte's health and capacity to face trial before setting a date for the start of the ICC trial
eyes 20-30 witnesses to testify orally in Duterte trial; over 13,000 evidence still pending review

key_actors.name: Senator Ronald 'Bato' Dela Rosa
key_actors.role: Senator, former chief enforcer of the Philippine drug war
key_actors.stated_position: Admits 'lapses' in the drug war, denies being like former President Duterte who wanted to surrender to the ICC, urged the military to stop government attempts to arrest him, filed petitions with the Supreme Court to block the ICC warrant, described as using the legislature as a shield.

key_actors.name: International Criminal Court (ICC)
key_actors.stated_position: Issued an arrest warrant for Senator Dela Rosa, added him to its list of wanted persons for alleged crimes against humanity, considers him a suspect-at-large, and its prosecution is eyeing witnesses and evidence for former President Duterte's trial.

key_actors.name: Office of the Solicitor General (OSG)
key_actors.stated_position: Opposes Senator Dela Rosa's Supreme Court petitions, arguing that justice should not forget the 'voiceless dead' and asserting the law must prevail to achieve justice for the deceased.

key_actors.name: Randy delos Santos
key_actors.role: Uncle of drug war victim Kian delos Santos
key_actors.stated_position: Calls on Senator Dela Rosa to come out and face the case.

key_actors.name: Atty. Israelito Torreon
key_actors.role: Legal counsel for Senator Ronald 'Bato' dela Rosa
key_actors.stated_position: States an immigration lookout bulletin order (ILBO) cannot prevent the senator from traveling abroad as it is not equivalent to a hold departure order.

key_actors.name: Department of Justice (DOJ)
key_actors.stated_position: Issued an immigration lookout bulletin order (ILBO) against Senator Bato dela Rosa to monitor his possible departure from the country.

key_actors.name: Former Senator Sonny Trillanes
key_actors.stated_position: Calls for a review of Senate Sergeant-at-Arms Mao Aplasca's background, citing his role in the Philippine National Police during the Duterte drug war and linking him to alleged extrajudicial killings (EJKs).

key_actors.name: Former President Rodrigo Duterte
key_actors.stated_position: Reportedly wanted to get arrested and surrender to the ICC, and his new lawyer is requesting a re-examination of his health and capacity to face trial before the ICC.

factual_claims.claim: Senator Ronald Dela Rosa is facing an ICC arrest warrant.
The ICC has added Senator Ronald Dela Rosa to its list of wanted persons.
The ICC arrest warrant against Senator Dela Rosa is for alleged crimes against humanity.
Police involved in the killing of Kian delos Santos were convicted.
The Department of Justice (DOJ) issued an immigration lookout bulletin order (ILBO) against Senator Bato dela Rosa.
The Office of the Solicitor General (OSG) submitted its comment on two petitions filed by Senator Ronald 'Bato' dela Rosa before the Supreme Court.
The OSG urged the Supreme Court to deny Senator Dela Rosa's petitions.
The OSG asked the Supreme Court to dismiss Senator Dela Rosa's petition to block the implementation of the ICC arrest warrant.
ICC prosecution plans to use 20-30 witnesses to testify orally in former President Duterte's trial.
Over 13,000 pieces of evidence are pending review for former President Duterte's trial.

disputed_claims.claim: Senator Dela Rosa actively resisted arrest, with shots fired.
disputed_claims.competing_accounts: RT_com reported 'SHOTS fired as Philippine top organizer of Duterte's drug war tries to resist his arrest' (J29), and Rich Heydarian mentioned 'SENATE SHOOTING' as part of 'surreal turn of events' (J34). However, other accounts only mention law enforcement chasing him inside the Senate building (J23) or reports of him leaving with a PNP escort (J41), without confirming shots fired or active resistance.

disputed_claims.claim: Senator Dela Rosa is a fugitive from justice.
disputed_claims.competing_accounts: The OSG stated Dela Rosa's actions placed him 'within the definition of fugitive' (J42), and a commentator suggested he 'should already be declared a fugitive from justice' (J50). Conversely, Dela Rosa's legal counsel stated an immigration lookout bulletin order (ILBO) is not equivalent to a hold departure order and cannot prevent his travel (J32), implying a legal basis for his movement.

competing_frames.frame: Accountability for alleged human rights abuses
competing_frames.proponents: ICC, Randy delos Santos, OSG, critics of Dela Rosa
competing_frames.summary: This frame emphasizes justice for victims of the drug war and adherence to international legal processes, advocating for Senator Dela Rosa to face charges.

competing_frames.frame: National sovereignty and resistance to international intervention
competing_frames.proponents: Senator Ronald Dela Rosa, public sentiment against foreign courts
competing_frames.summary: This frame emphasizes the primacy of domestic legal processes and resistance to external jurisdiction, viewing international legal action as an infringement on national authority.

competing_frames.frame: Political maneuvering and impunity for powerful figures
competing_frames.proponents: Critics of Dela Rosa, commentators on political actions
competing_frames.summary: This frame suggests that Senator Dela Rosa is using his political office and alleged 'official protection' to evade justice, highlighting perceived systemic failures in holding powerful individuals accountable.
